---
title: Pesto from garden basil
date: 2024-07-22
tags: [cooking, gardening, italian, herbs]
---

The basil bed outside the kitchen door is my favorite shortcut from garden to table. Genovese basil has broad, soft leaves and a clean perfume that lettuce-leaf varieties cannot match. I pinch flowering tips constantly from June onward so the plants stay leafy instead of going to seed.

For pesto I work in a marble mortar when I have the time. Garlic and coarse salt go in first, then pine nuts, then the basil leaves a handful at a time. Once everything is a rough paste I stir in grated Parmigiano and Pecorino and enough olive oil to loosen it.

A food processor works, but the blades bruise the basil and turn it darker. Either way I pour a thin layer of oil on top of the jar to keep the color bright. In a good year the freezer fills with small containers that carry the smell of July into January.
